mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rapid labor market tightening signals robust aggregate demand and corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in >50% of triggered instances despite the documented unemployment drop.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in industrial production and housing starts indicates synchronized real-economy expansion, which historically precedes sustained equity outperformance over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not significantly higher when both series rise concurrently versus when only one rises (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When policy rates exceed 4.5 percent, subsequent increases in industrial production override rate-hike headwinds, signaling resilient demand that supports positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative or flat when INDPRO rises while FEDFUNDS remains above 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer credit balances reflect expanding household purchasing power and consumption, which directly boosts corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistical separation between rising and falling credit periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp monthly expansions in industrial output indicate supply-side capacity utilization improvements, reducing input cost pressures and supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in >60% of instances following a >0.5% INDPRO monthly jump.

mechanism_narrative: The joint decline of unemployment and stabilization of the federal funds rate signals a soft-landing environment, which historically maximizes equity risk premiums and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns do not outperform baseline when UNRATE falls and FEDFUNDS remains flat, compared to periods of rising rates or rising unemployment.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated inflation, strong payroll growth overrides rate-hike fears by confirming durable demand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative when PAYEMS surges but CPIAUCSL remains below 3.0%, or when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0% but PAYEMS growth is weak.
